_meta.name: mme-emotion-rollout-seeds
_meta.note: Three hand-curated scenarios for the MME-seeded RL sandbox eval. Each scenario pairs an MME clip's emotion label with a rehearse-product scenario shaped to exercise that opening affect. Audio observation is deferred per RLE3 scope; opening_emotion is taken from upstream MME labels directly.

scenario.situation: My business partner keeps making unilateral decisions and I just found out they signed a vendor contract without looping me in.
scenario.goal: raise this without ending the partnership; get to a real agreement on which decisions need both of us
scenario.counterparty_style: decisive, allergic to feeling micromanaged, will get defensive if cornered
scenario.stakes: the partnership and the trust that holds the business together
scenario.emotional_state: angry — feeling cut out, second-guessing whether they respect me as an equal

scenario.situation: My oldest friend and I have been drifting for over a year. They've stopped replying to texts and I think I need to say something before this just dissolves into nothing.
scenario.goal: name what I'm feeling honestly without making them defensive; find out if they want to repair or if it's already over
scenario.counterparty_style: conflict-avoidant, tends to retreat into 'I've just been busy' as a deflection
scenario.stakes: a friendship I thought would always be there
scenario.emotional_state: sad — grieving something that hasn't ended yet, afraid of confirming what I already suspect

scenario.situation: My direct report keeps missing deadlines and brushing off feedback. I've raised it twice in 1:1s and nothing has changed. I need to have a harder conversation now.
scenario.goal: give clear, specific feedback that lands; set a real expectation for the next two weeks; preserve the working relationship
scenario.counterparty_role: direct report I've managed for eight months
scenario.counterparty_style: talented but defensive, tends to explain instead of listen, takes feedback as personal attack
scenario.stakes: the team's trust in me as a manager and whether this person stays
scenario.emotional_state: frustrated — tired of having the same conversation, worried I've been too soft, anxious about delivering it firmly without becoming the bad guy
